results.name: CH5.1: sin(theta) Taylor: linear coefficient = 1
CH5.2: sin(theta) Taylor: cubic coefficient = -1/6
CH5.3: Characteristic roots are +/- i*sqrt(g/l)
CH5.4: A*cos(wt)+B*sin(wt) satisfies ddot(theta)+w^2*theta=0
CH5.5: Solution with omega=sqrt(g/l) satisfies linearized pendulum
CH9.1: Covariant derivative transforms as D'psi' = e^{i*alpha}*D*psi
CH9.2: F_munu is gauge-invariant (F_01' = F_01)
CH13.1: dL/dqdot = m*qdot (canonical momentum)
CH13.2: dL/dq = -V'(q) (Euler-Lagrange force term)
CH13.3: Free particle classical action S = m(xb-xa)^2 / (2(tb-ta))
CH13.4: Propagator exponent = i*m*(xb-xa)^2 / (2*hbar*(tb-ta))
CH17.1: Fixed points are x=0, x=+/-sqrt(r)
CH17.4: f'(sqrt(r)) = -2r
CH17.6: At r=0, only fixed point is x=0 (triple root)
CH17.7: For r<0 (r=-1), only x=0 is real fixed point
C1.1: Babylonian sequence for sqrt(2) is Cauchy (differences decrease)
C1.2: a_n^2 -> 2 (sequence converges to sqrt(2))
C1.3: Two sequences for sqrt(2) are equivalent (|a_n - b_n| -> 0)
C1.4: Harmonic partial sums are NOT Cauchy (|a_20 - a_10| > 1/2)
C2.1: x^2 + 1 = 0 solvable in C
C2.3: z^5 + z + 1 has 5 roots in C (fundamental theorem of algebra)
C2.4: All roots of z^5 + z + 1 satisfy the equation
C2.5: x^2 + 1 has no real roots (R not algebraically closed)
C2.6: C cannot be an ordered field (structural)
results.detail: If i>0 then i^2=-1>0, contradiction. If i<0 then (-i)^2=-1>0, contradiction. Orderability destroyed by algebraic closure, as claimed.

results.name: C3.1: Universal property of Stone-Cech compactification
results.detail: For all compact Hausdorff K, for all continuous f: X -> K, there exists unique continuous beta(f): beta(X) -> K with beta(f) o iota = f. This is the defining universal property (category-theoretic left adjoint to forgetful functor).

results.name: C3.2: beta(X) is compact Hausdorff (by construction)
results.detail: Stone-Cech compactification is constructed as closure of image in product of [0,1]^C(X,[0,1]). Product of compact Hausdorff spaces is compact Hausdorff (Tychonoff). Closed subspace of compact Hausdorff is compact Hausdorff.

results.name: C3.3: beta(X) is maximal compactification
results.detail: For any compactification gamma(X) of X, there exists a continuous surjection beta(X) -> gamma(X). This follows from the universal property applied to the embedding X -> gamma(X).

results.name: C3.4: Chain decomposes as EXTEND (embed) -> COMPLETE (compact closure) -> MAP (extension)
results.detail: EXTEND: X embeds into beta(X). COMPLETE: beta(X) is the completion under compactness. MAP: continuous functions extend uniquely. Matches ChatGPT's claimed chain structure.

results.name: C4.1: a * a^{-1} = identity (word cancellation)
C4.2: a*b*b^{-1}*a^{-1} = identity (nested cancellation)
C4.3: a*b*a is NOT identity (irreducible word)
C4.4: Free group is non-abelian (a*b != b*a)
C4.5: Universal mapping property of free group
results.detail: For any group G and set map f: S -> G, there exists a unique homomorphism phi: F(S) -> G such that phi(s) = f(s) for all s in S. This is the defining property: F is left adjoint to the forgetful functor Grp -> Set.

results.name: C4.6: Associativity: (a*b)*(a*b^{-1}) = a*(b*a)*b^{-1}
C5.1: Every R-module has an injective resolution
results.detail: By Baer's criterion, every module embeds into an injective module. Iterate: 0 -> A -> I^0 -> I^1 -> ... is an injective resolution. This is the EXTEND step in the chain.

results.name: C5.2: Left-exact functor F applied to resolution gives cochain complex
results.detail: F left-exact means F preserves kernels (0 -> A -> B exact implies 0 -> FA -> FB exact). Applying F to 0 -> I^0 -> I^1 -> ... gives 0 -> F(I^0) -> F(I^1) -> ... This is a cochain complex. This is the MAP step.

results.name: C5.3: R^nF(A) = H^n(F(I^*)) (derived functor = cohomology of applied resolution)
results.detail: R^nF(A) = ker(F(I^n) -> F(I^{n+1})) / im(F(I^{n-1}) -> F(I^n)). This is the REDUCE step: quotient by coboundaries.

results.name: C5.4: Derived functor is independent of choice of resolution
results.detail: Any two injective resolutions are chain homotopy equivalent (comparison theorem). Homotopy equivalent complexes have isomorphic cohomology. This is the COMPLETE step: uniqueness guarantees well-definedness.

results.name: C5.5: Chain structure EXTEND -> MAP -> REDUCE -> COMPLETE confirmed
results.detail: EXTEND: construct resolution. MAP: apply functor F. REDUCE: take cohomology (quotient). COMPLETE: uniqueness (resolution-independence). Matches the claimed chain: resolution -> apply F -> cohomology -> universal delta-functor.

results.name: C5.6: Ext^1_Z(Z/nZ, Z) = Z/nZ (concrete derived functor computation)
results.detail: Resolution: 0 -> Z -> Z -> Z/nZ -> 0 (multiplication by n). Apply Hom(-,Z): 0 -> Z -> Z -> coker -> 0. Cokernel of (x n): Z -> Z is Z/nZ. Hence Ext^1(Z/nZ, Z) = Z/nZ.
